description_long: This object is a large, cylindrical bomb with a heavily rusted, weathered metal exterior, giving it an aged and industrial appearance. The main body is a thick-walled cylinder, capped at both ends, with three prominent triangular stabilizing fins attached near the rear, each showing signs of corrosion and wear. The front end is open, revealing the hollow interior, while the rear end is closed and features a few small protruding elements, possibly for mounting or triggering. Several metal loops and brackets are attached to the body, likely for handling or securing the device. The surface is uniformly covered in a reddish-brown rust, with no visible paint, markings, or warning labels. The overall construction suggests a robust, utilitarian design typical of older, unguided aerial bombs.
description: A large, rusted cylindrical bomb with three triangular stabilizing fins and an open front end, showing heavy weathering and industrial construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open, hollow end of the bomb and two of the three large triangular fins.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical body, all three fins, and various metal loops and brackets.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed end of the bomb and the symmetrical arrangement of the fins.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with visible fins and surface details.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No explicit detonation mechanism is visible; possible triggering could occur via mechanical or impact-based means, suggested by the presence of brackets and loops.
